sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Lotus Technology (NASDAQ:LOT) Shares Gap Down - What's Next?
feed.summary: Lotus Technology (NASDAQ:LOT) shares gapped down at market open to $1.41, trading around $1.46 on light volume, following previous close at $1.49. Analysts remain bearish with a consensus "Sell" rating, despite the company reporting a narrower-than-expected loss per share and revenue that beat estimates. Institutional investors have recently adjusted their positions in the company.

feed.title: Kodak Stages Remarkable Comeback Under New CEO
feed.summary: Kodak's shares have surged nearly 100% in the past year due to CEO Jim Continenza's strategy of debt reduction and film revival. The company, which faced "going-concern" issues in 2025, has successfully transformed from near bankruptcy to profitability by leveraging its core strengths and brand recognition, notably its film heritage. This turnaround has been supported by renewed interest from Hollywood and younger generations in analog formats.

feed.title: Following layoffs, Oracle pursues zoning change for East Bank campus
feed.summary: Despite recent large-scale layoffs and economic shifts due to AI, Oracle is moving forward with its planned multibillion-dollar East Bank campus development in Nashville. The company is pursuing a zoning change for its River North properties and has informed the Metro Council that it intends to establish its global headquarters in Nashville. This comes as some local politicians express skepticism about the project, which was initially promised to bring 8,500 high-paying jobs.

feed.title: Disney reports quarterly results May 6 before market open
feed.summary: The Walt Disney Company (NYSE: DIS) will host a live webcast to discuss its fiscal second quarter 2026 financial results on Wednesday, May 6, 2026, at 8:30 a.m. ET. The company plans to release its earnings results and post related materials on its investor relations website before the market opens on that day. Interested parties can access the webcast and materials at www.disney.com/investors.

feed.title: Molina Healthcare, MolinaCares Accord Award $120,000 to Support Senior Wellness Programs
feed.summary: Molina Healthcare and The MolinaCares Accord have granted $120,000 to Hebrew SeniorLife to fund fitness and behavioral health programs in senior living communities across Brockton, Cambridge, and Medford. This initiative aims to improve seniors' physical, mental, and social well-being by providing on-site services that overcome common barriers like transportation. The funding builds on previous investments, reinforcing a commitment to whole-person care and supporting older adults in aging with dignity.

feed.title: Air Products and Chemicals (NYSE:APD) Stock Price Expected to Rise, Citigroup Analyst Says
feed.summary: Citigroup has raised its price target for Air Products and Chemicals (NYSE:APD) to $315, maintaining a "neutral" rating, indicating a potential upside of 5.61%. This follows other upgrades from JPMorgan and Wells Fargo, contributing to a "Moderate Buy" consensus among analysts with an average price target of $303.94. The company recently surpassed quarterly earnings and revenue expectations, and institutional investors have significantly increased their holdings.

feed.title: Coke releasing state of Alabama can for America’s 250th birthday: Can you guess what image is on it?
feed.summary: Coca-Cola is celebrating America's 250th anniversary by releasing collectible mini-Coke cans for each state, alongside public art projects and community volunteer initiatives. The Alabama can will feature a football helmet set amid a silver star, reflecting its college football culture. The campaign also includes interactive packaging with prizes and aims to generate 250,000 volunteer hours nationwide.

feed.title: Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) Stock Price Expected to Rise, Citigroup Analyst Says
feed.summary: Citigroup has raised its price target for Western Digital (NASDAQ:WDC) to $405 from $335, reiterating a "buy" rating, which suggests a potential upside of 16.7%. This revision follows Western Digital's strong quarterly performance, where it surpassed earnings and revenue expectations. The stock is currently trading near its 12-month high, reflecting positive market sentiment and robust institutional ownership.

feed.title: Hattersley Gavin buys McCormick & Co (MKC) stock worth $105960 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Gavin Hattersley, a Director at McCormick & Co Inc (NYSE:MKC), purchased 2,000 shares of the company's non-voting common stock for $105,960. This transaction increases his direct ownership to 406 shares of voting common stock and 2,906 shares of non-voting common stock. The stock purchase follows McCormick's strong Q1 2026 financial results, which included an EPS beat and higher-than-expected revenue, despite investor concerns over a merger with Unilever Foods and a lowered price target from BofA Securities.

feed.title: Tractor Supply Co. store being renovated in Porter
feed.summary: The Tractor Supply Co. store in Porter is currently undergoing a renovation that is expected to be completed by the end of April. The project will expand the store's sales floor to accommodate more products and create additional space between departments. The store will remain open throughout the renovation period.

feed.title: More production employees called back by John Deere
feed.summary: John Deere announced it is calling back nearly 50 additional employees to three Midwest manufacturing plants, including Davenport Works, Dubuque Works, and Coffeyville Works. These callbacks support growing production needs in construction, forestry, and drivetrain component operations. This latest action brings the total number of U.S. employees returning to work since January to 324, reflecting improving market conditions and increased production demand.

feed.title: Abbott Laboratories stock edges lower as Team Abbott spotlights runner's Boston Marathon story
feed.summary: Abbott Laboratories stock (ABT) edged lower by 0.54% today, trading at $99.76. The company recently shared a Boston Marathon runner's story as part of its "Team Abbott" initiative, while technical indicators point to persistent bearish momentum, with ABT trading below key moving averages and momentum indicators signaling oversold conditions. Analysts predict consolidation between $97.75 and $101.75 in the coming week, with a high likelihood of further declines.

feed.title: 3 Retail Stocks to Watch for a Post-Tax-Day Bump
feed.summary: As Tax Day passes, some retailers may see a boost from consumers spending their refunds. This article highlights three retail stocks – Target (TGT), Deckers Outdoor (DECK), and Best Buy (BBY) – that could experience short-term gains. Target needs a sales win after facing various challenges, Deckers is capitalizing on market share gains with brands like UGG and HOKA, and Best Buy could benefit if refund dollars are used for big-ticket electronics.

feed.title: What's Going On With NIO Stock Wednesday?
feed.summary: NIO stock is trading lower following a price target reduction by BofA Securities to $5.90. Despite this, the company is expanding its presence in Europe with a new NIO House in the Netherlands and continues to innovate in battery technology, with trial operations commencing for its 150-kWh semi-solid-state battery. China is also investing significantly in R&D for all-solid-state batteries, benefiting other key players in the EV sector.

feed.title: Rosen Law Firm Encourages Luda Technology Group Ltd. Investors to Inquire About Securities Class Action Investigation – LUD
feed.summary: The Rosen Law Firm is investigating potential securities claims against Luda Technology Group Ltd. (NYSE American: LUD) due to allegations of materially misleading business information. Investors who purchased Luda securities may be entitled to compensation and are encouraged to contact the Rosen Law Firm for information on joining a prospective class action lawsuit. The firm highlights its experience and success in securities class action litigation.

feed.title: $4 Gas Is Squeezing Consumers — Why Pizza Stocks Could Gain - Domino's Pizza (NASDAQ:DPZ), Papa John's In
feed.summary: A Bank of America research note indicates that when gasoline prices rise, consumers reallocate spending, generally cutting back on expensive dining but increasing spending on quick-service restaurants (QSRs), especially pizza chains. This shift benefits companies like Domino's Pizza, McDonald's, Yum! Brands, and Papa John's as consumers trade down to more affordable meal options. The report suggests that while restaurant spending hasn't fully registered this shift yet, the sustained high gas prices are likely to drive consumers towards QSRs.

feed.title: Public Storage (NYSE: PSA) director reports 12,000-share gift transfers
feed.summary: Public Storage director Avedick B. Poladian reported transferring a total of 12,000 common shares as bona fide gifts on April 10, 2026. These transfers included 2,000 shares each to three AMMEA trusts, with corresponding adjustments to his direct holdings. After these transactions, Poladian directly holds 24,163 shares and indirectly holds shares through the trusts and an IRA.

feed.title: What is behind AbbVie stock's recent drop in value today
feed.summary: AbbVie Inc. (ABBV) stock dropped 2.20% today to $203.48, trading below all key moving averages despite positive Phase 2 clinical trial results for an ovarian cancer treatment and expanding its pipeline with a new licensing deal. Technical analysis shows strong bearish momentum and oversold conditions, with experts advising caution against bottom-fishing. While short-term pressure persists, some analysts believe the company's strong pipeline and fundamental health could present opportunities for forward-looking investors.

feed.title: This Week in Wake Forest Women’s Athletics – Presented by Truist (April 13-19)
feed.summary: This article provides an update on Wake Forest Women's Athletics for the week of April 13-19, presented by Truist. It highlights the achievements of the Track & Field team, including record-breaking performances in various events, and the Women's Tennis team's progression to the ACC Championships after closing their regular season with a win against Virginia Tech. The piece also includes information about the sponsor, Truist, and Wake Forest University.

feed.title: McCormick (NYSE: MKC) director adds 2,000 non-voting shares in open-market buy
feed.summary: McCormick & Co. director Gavin Hattersley purchased 2,000 shares of McCormick non-voting common stock on April 10, 2026, at $52.98 per share in an open-market transaction. Following this acquisition, Hattersley directly holds 2,906 non-voting shares and 406 voting shares. This insider buy signals a positive sentiment from a key executive regarding the company's prospects.

feed.title: Decision Science Advisors, LLC announced that it has received funding from Jefferies Financial Group Inc.
feed.summary: Decision Science Advisors, LLC has secured funding from Jefferies Financial Group Inc. in a round that saw Jefferies Financial Group Inc. participating as a new investor. The transaction involved the issuance of convertible preferred stock. This investment was officially announced on April 13, 2026.

feed.title: Activist Investor Third Point Reportedly Sells CoStar Group Position
feed.summary: Activist investor Third Point has reportedly sold its entire stake in CoStar Group, terminating its proxy fight. Third Point's founder Daniel Loeb stated that the firm no longer believes its original thesis holds true due to CEO Andy Florance's continued investment in Homes.com despite a falling share price. The spat began in January over CoStar's strategy for Homes.com, which Third Point criticized as an expensive flop with high executive compensation.

feed.title: Arista Networks Drives AI Ethernet Revenue Growth
feed.summary: Arista Networks is strengthening its position in AI Ethernet networking, with its AI segment projected to double and exceed 30% of total sales this year. The company boasts high profitability, a robust backlog, and strong relationships with major hyperscalers like Meta and Microsoft, supported by its EOS operating system and optical innovations. Despite recent share price gains, the current valuation is considered reasonable for a long-term investment due to its strong cash flow, customer ties, and product differentiation.

feed.title: Analyst mixes Vegas encouragement with caution
feed.summary: Truist Securities analyst Barry Jonas provided an investor note on the Las Vegas market, highlighting that while April room rates are "soft," May and June show solidification. He cautioned about a "volatile" macroeconomic picture and a K-shaped recovery favoring high-end consumers. Jonas noted improved first-quarter room rates towards the end of the quarter and forecasted a possible positive RevPAR inflection for the summer despite April's struggles.

feed.title: A Look At Inventiva (ENXTPA:IVA) Valuation After Weaker Full Year 2025 Earnings
feed.summary: Inventiva (ENXTPA:IVA) reported weaker full-year 2025 earnings with a net loss of €354.14 million, despite sales of €4.48 million. Analysts currently value the company at €10.00 per share, citing potential future revenue from its MASH program, but this is tempered by falling revenues, wider losses, and reliance on lanifibranor. Investors are advised to review the data themselves, considering both the potential upside and significant risks associated with the company's financial performance and clinical trials.

feed.title: MSCI buys PM Insights to scale private market data
feed.summary: MSCI has acquired PM Insights, a specialist data firm focused on private company securities, to expand its private market coverage. This acquisition adds secondary market reference data to MSCI's platform, addressing the increasing investor demand for transparency in private assets.

feed.title: Here’s when this growing area near Columbia will get a new Chipotle restaurant
feed.summary: A new Chipotle Mexican Grill location is set to open on April 14, 2026, at 1069 Dutch Fork Road in Irmo, South Carolina. The restaurant will feature a drive-thru "Chipotlane" and special limited-time menu items. This opening further develops the bustling commercial corridor of Dutch Fork Road, which sees significant daily traffic and already hosts several other businesses.

feed.title: Should Li Auto’s March Delivery Rebound and Tech Push Require Action From Li Auto (LI) Investors?
feed.summary: Li Auto reported a significant rebound in March 2026 deliveries with 41,053 vehicles and 1.64 million cumulative deliveries, largely due to the resolved Li i6 production bottlenecks. Alongside this operational success, the company is advancing in autonomous driving technology, expanding ESG reporting, and executing a share repurchase program. Investors need to weigh these positive developments against the ongoing challenges of intense market competition, profit pressures from lower average selling prices, and substantial R&D and capital expenditure requirements.

feed.title: Decision Science Advisors Secures Series A to Scale AI for Private Equity
feed.summary: Decision Science Advisors (DSA) has successfully raised a Series A funding round led by Jefferies Financial Group to expand its applied AI advisory services within the US and Europe. The company specializes in bridging the gap between AI strategy and execution for private equity firms and their portfolio companies. This funding will enable DSA to scale its operations and impact, addressing the growing demand for practical AI implementations in enterprise and private equity sectors.

feed.title: McDonald's Insider Sold Shares Worth $806,182, According to a Recent SEC Filing
feed.summary: An insider at McDonald's Corporation sold shares valued at $806,182, as reported in a recent SEC filing. The article highlights this transaction and provides other recent news and financial data related to McDonald's, including its stock performance and analyst ratings.

feed.title: FSLR.SW stock surges 144% in after-hours trading on SIX
feed.summary: First Solar, Inc. (FSLR.SW) experienced a significant 144% surge in after-hours trading on the SIX exchange, reaching CHF 153.40. This dramatic increase makes it a high-volume mover, driven by strong investor interest in renewable energy and positive technical indicators. Meyka AI assigned FSLR.SW a B+ rating with a BUY recommendation, citing solid fundamentals and growth prospects despite a premium valuation.

feed.title: Jefferies Adjusts Price Target on NRG Energy to $199 From $181, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Jefferies has increased its price target for NRG Energy (NRG) to $199 from $181, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment comes as part of ongoing analyst recommendations for the electric utilities company. Other recent analyst actions include Morgan Stanley adjusting its target to $157 with an Equalweight rating, and BNP Paribas initiating coverage with an Outperform rating and a $232 price target.

feed.title: Tesla’s Ex-President Boosted Sales With a Trick Learned From Domino’s Pizza
feed.summary: Tesla's former president, Jon McNeill, implemented a strategy learned from Domino's Pizza to significantly boost online car sales. Challenged by Elon Musk to increase digital sales by 2,000 percent, McNeill realized that Tesla's complex ordering process, with 64 clicks, caused decision fatigue. By simplifying the car configurations to offer only 2-3 preconfigured models and reducing customization, he cut the clicks needed to purchase, making the process much more streamlined and user-friendly, similar to Domino's 10-click ordering system.

feed.title: Jefferies Adjusts Price Target on Vulcan Materials to $360 From $375, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Jefferies has adjusted its price target for Vulcan Materials Company (VMC) from $375 to $360, while reiterating a "Buy" rating on the stock. This update comes as other financial institutions like BNP Paribas and Morgan Stanley have also recently updated their price targets and ratings for the construction materials company. Vulcan Materials specializes in aggregates, asphalt, and concrete, primarily operating in the United States.

feed.title: Jefferies & Co Adjusts Martin Marietta Materials Price Target to $745 From $785, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Jefferies & Co has adjusted its price target for Martin Marietta Materials (MLM) to $745, down from $785, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst other recent analyst re-evaluations, including BNP Paribas, Morgan Stanley, and Barclays, who have also modified their price targets for the company. Martin Marietta Materials recently updated its earnings guidance for 2026 and completed an acquisition of Aggregates Operations.

feed.title: Autonomous Research Cuts Price Target on T. Rowe Price Group to $79 From $83, Maintains Underperform Rating
feed.summary: Autonomous Research has reduced its price target for T. Rowe Price Group (TROW) to $79 from $83, while maintaining an "Underperform" rating on the stock. This news comes as T. Rowe Price also reported lower Assets Under Management (AUM) in March. The article lists several other analysts who have recently adjusted their price targets for T. Rowe Price Group.

feed.title: Imperial Petroleum (IMPP) price target increased by 33.33% to 8.16
feed.summary: The article states that Imperial Petroleum's (IMPP) price target has been increased by 33.33%, bringing it to $8.16. No further details or context are provided.

feed.title: LM Funding America Announces March 2026 Production Update
feed.summary: LM Funding America, a Bitcoin treasury and cryptocurrency mining company, has released its March 2026 production and operational update. As of March 31, 2026, the company's Bitcoin treasury stood at 341.2 BTC, valued at $22.9 million or $1.07 per share. This update highlights the company's strategic focus on cryptocurrency mining and a growing digital asset management strategy.

feed.title: Klarna (KLAR) Chief Product Officer granted 117,163 options at $13.04 strike
feed.summary: Klarna Group plc's Chief Product & Design Officer, David Fock, was granted 117,163 stock options with an exercise price of $13.04 per share and an expiration date of September 30, 2030. This compensation-related grant increases his total derivative holdings to 1,864,999 options. The transaction was reported via an SEC Form 4 filing on April 13, 2026.

feed.title: Lucid Recalls 3,627 Air Pure EVs Over Half-Shaft Bolt Defect
feed.summary: Lucid is recalling 3,627 Air Pure RWD vehicles from model years 2024–2026 due to half-shaft bolts that may disconnect from the drive unit, leading to a complete loss of power. This recall expands on a previous action and affects vehicles produced between September 2023 and July 2025. Lucid plans to replace the bolts and implement an over-the-air software update to warn drivers of potential disengagement.

feed.title: 20 employees to be brought back to John Deere's Davenport Works
feed.summary: John Deere is recalling 41 workers to its Davenport and Dubuque facilities in Iowa, including 20 to Davenport Works, and an additional 8 to Coffeyville Works in Kansas. These callbacks bring the total number of employees rehired since January to 324, signaling improving market conditions and increased production demand for the company. Employees are returning throughout April, with some already back in March.

feed.title: Are Investors Undervaluing Lear (LEA) Right Now?
feed.summary: Lear (LEA) has been identified by Zacks as a strong value stock, currently holding a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) and an 'A' grade for Value. Its valuation metrics, including a P/E ratio of 7.87 and a PEG ratio of 0.75, are significantly lower than its industry averages, suggesting the stock may be undervalued. This analysis, combined with a positive earnings outlook, indicates that LEA is an attractive investment for value investors.

feed.title: Higher NII & Loan Growth to Support U.S. Bancorp's Q1 Earnings
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp (USB) is expected to report increased revenues and earnings for the first quarter of 2026, driven by higher net interest income (NII) and loan growth. The company recently agreed to acquire BTIG, LLC to expand its capital markets capabilities. While NII is projected to rise due to stable interest rates, and loan demand is expected to improve, the mortgage banking business might see a decline due to elevated rates and low affordability.

feed.title: J.P.Morgan, Morgan Stanley urge buying the dip as US earnings stay resilient
feed.summary: J.P.Morgan and Morgan Stanley recommend buying market dips, citing resilient U.S. corporate earnings growth despite geopolitical tensions stemming from the Middle East conflict. The S&P 500 has shown strength, outperforming European and emerging markets, with earnings growth estimates increasing for Q1 2026. Both firms highlight opportunities in cyclical sectors and quality growth stocks, even as their regional preferences for equities differ.

feed.title: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC Urges Enphase Energy, Inc. Investors to Act: Class Action Filed Alleging Investor Harm
feed.summary: Bronstein, Gewirtz & Grossman LLC has filed a class action lawsuit against Enphase Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: ENPH) alleging violations of federal securities laws. The lawsuit claims Enphase overstated its ability to manage channel inventory and mitigate effects from the termination of the 25D Credit, thereby overstating its financial and operational prospects. Investors who purchased Enphase securities between April 22, 2025, and October 28, 2025, are encouraged to join the case, with a lead plaintiff deadline of April 20, 2026.

feed.title: Bernstein Adjusts PT on EOG Resources to $167 From $126, Maintains Market Perform Rating
feed.summary: Bernstein has increased its price target for EOG Resources from $126 to $167, while maintaining a Market Perform rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects an updated outlook for the oil and gas exploration and production company. The article also lists other recent analyst adjustments for EOG Resources.

feed.title: Bernstein Adjusts Price Target on ConocoPhillips to $121 From $98, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Bernstein has increased its price target for ConocoPhillips to $121 from $98, while reaffirming an Outperform rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst news of fluctuating market conditions and geopolitical events impacting the energy sector. The article also provides a company profile for ConocoPhillips, outlining its specialization in hydrocarbon exploration and production.

feed.title: Higher NII & Loan Growth to Support U.S. Bancorp's Q1 Earnings
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp (USB) is projected to report increased revenues and earnings for the first quarter of 2026, driven by higher net interest income (NII) and loan growth. The company recently agreed to acquire BTIG, LLC to expand its capital markets capabilities. Despite a strong outlook for NII and capital markets revenue, the mortgage banking business is expected to face challenges due to high rates and low affordability, and the Zacks model indicates a low chance of exceeding earnings estimates.

feed.title: Autodesk : appoints Mike Kelly as Chief Information Officer
feed.summary: Autodesk has appointed Mike Kelly as its new Chief Information Officer (CIO), effective April 13, 2026. Kelly will lead enterprise technology strategy, AI adoption, and digital employee experience, aligning with Autodesk's goals to boost productivity, strengthen its technology foundation, and accelerate innovation. He brings extensive experience from previous CIO roles at Andreessen Horowitz (a16z), Red Hat, and McKesson.

feed.title: UBS Raises Price Target on Pfizer to $27 From $25, Maintains Neutral Rating
feed.summary: UBS has increased its price target for Pfizer (PFE) to $27 from $25, while retaining a Neutral rating on the stock. This adjustment comes amidst ongoing market activity and a range of analyst opinions on the pharmaceutical giant.
